element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Hub area contains 16 cave entrances player can access in any order
Player begins with 5 rockets; must manage ammo across level
Simple puzzle solving—pull lever to open corresponding door
Destroyed with rocket gun; avoidance or combat required
Toggle obstacles on/off to navigate or create safe passages
Destroys all enemies with single shot; temporary duration
Allows walking on ceiling; spatial disorientation and platforming challenge
Freezes all enemies on screen; tactical evasion mechanic
Resource management; collect to replenish ammo pool
Collectible goal; must obtain all to complete game

typical_relief_triggers: Activating Stop Signs for safe passage
Securing rocket pickups to restore ammo
typical_tension_triggers: Time pressure from level design (implied by level quit penalty)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Moderate pace with non-linear progression. Player can quit caves at will (no checkpoints mid-level), creating episodic tension cycles. Tension releases upon crystal collection or safe exit. Hit point loss (3 HP total) creates resource scarcity similar to ammo management.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Color-coded doors likely signal optional branches; player can access caves in any order from hub

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low friction introduction; basic movement, simple lever puzzles, manageable enemy encounters. Players learn core mechanics in hub and introductory caves.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Complexity increases with switch-based obstacle sequences, enemy density, and power-up introduction (P-Pill, Stop Signs). Resource management (rockets/health) becomes strategic.
difficulty_curve.late_game: G-Pill gravity-reversal mechanics introduce spatial disorientation. Late caves likely combine multiple puzzle elements (color doors + switches + enemies). Ammo and health conservation critical.
notable_spikes: Caves combining color-key puzzles with enemy gauntlets
Final caves requiring full powerup roster awareness

notable_level_patterns.description: Main level contains 16 cave entrances. Player chooses cave order freely. Quitting a cave negates progress but allows reordering priority. This creates self-directed pacing and optional difficulty scaling.
Fundamental puzzle archetype: pull colored lever to unlock matching door. Spatial memory and switch-tracking required. Can be layered with enemy encounters for tension.
Caves structuring player ammo and health conservation. Enemy density and layout force players to choose between combat and evasion. Stop Signs and P-Pills provide tactical relief valves.
G-Pill mechanic inverts spatial cognition. Ceiling platforms become floor platforms. Introduces vertical disorientation without changing fundamental platforming verbs.
